page: The Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-75s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring two shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor operating at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 4.0 GB of system memory, equating to 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 75 GB NVMe SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark data reveals a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2929.97. However, multi-core performance is weak, scoring 5812.19 in stress-ng and 4823.64 in the PassMark CPU test. Memory performance is similarly constrained; the PassMark memory score of 1687.01 and read-only memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the bottom 25%, though large block write bandwidth remains average at 34.24 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, demonstrate average tier performance.

Due to its shared CPU allocation, this instance exhibits lower multi-threaded efficiency and database performance, with PassMark database operations and static web serving throughput ranking in the bottom 10%. This resource density makes the server a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require sustained multi-core utilization. It is appropriate for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic utility hosting, and low-traffic web serving, but is poorly configured for high-throughput databases or intensive multi-threaded applications.
description: The Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-75s-amd is an entry-level instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, utilizing two shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4.0 GB of RAM, a 75 GB NVMe SSD, and one public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core CPU capability, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. However, the shared CPU allocation results in weak multi-core and memory performance, with PassMark CPU and memory scores falling in the bottom 25%, and database operations and static web serving throughput dropping to the bottom 10%. This server offers a budget-conscious option for workloads that rely on single-threaded execution rather than parallel processing, making it appropriate for lightweight utility applications, basic single-threaded tasks, and low-traffic web hosting.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-75s-amd is a shared AMD EPYC-Rome instance with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and 75 GB NVMe storage, offering strong single-core performance alongside budget-friendly shared resources.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-75s-amd is a shared AMD EPYC server with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and a 75 GB NVMe SSD, designed for budget-friendly basic workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared AMD EPYC instance featuring strong single-core performance and integrated NVMe storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Two shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth performance due to shared allocation
Appropriate for lightweight single-threaded applications and basic web hosting
